question.text: Which of the following is the BEST way to evaluate a mutual fund's performance?

answer.text: Compare it with its benchmark and similar category funds over multiple time periods
Look at only last month's return
Choose the fund with the highest NAV
Pick the fund with the most advertisements

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The best evaluation method is to compare the fund's returns with its benchmark AND with peer funds in the same category, across multiple time periods (1 year, 3 years, 5 years). This gives a comprehensive view of performance and consistency.
**The Trap:** One month's return is too short. High NAV does not mean better performance. Advertisements are marketing, not performance indicators.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. Multi-period, multi-comparison evaluation is the gold standard.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use the "3-check" approach: (1) Compare with benchmark, (2) Compare with category peers, (3) Check consistency across time periods. This gives a robust performance picture.
